strings.tag: Hi [name], this is a test string from room002.
Do you have a question for me?
Doc Robbins' team has already collected it. He's probably already started the autopsy.
Have you talked with Ms. Ranger to see what she knows? Also, you need to search this room for evidence.
Not in this case. Part of the agreement to enter the dog show was to give the show access to search all property. We've got the permission to search everything and everywhere.
Not quite. I think you should ask Ms. Ranger about the schedule.
I think there's more work to do.
Yes. Let's move on to the Morgue and see how Dr. Robbins can help us.
Where's the dead body?
What should I do here?
Are we finished here?
Don't we need a warrant or her permission to search this place?
I'm going to ask her now.
I'll keep investigating.
I'm going to the Morgue now.
Where's my Apollo? Do you have any news about my Apollo?
Apollo! He's my prize-winning pure-bred Cocker Spaniel, and if he's to win Best in Show you have to find him NOW! He's scheduled to compete in three hours!
My fabulous dog had been stolen! A crime has occurred, but no one's doing anything about it! You have to catch the dog-napper!
Marco Williams? He's Apollo's handler. He looks after Apollo before and during the competitions. Obviously, he failed to protect Apollo from the dog-nappers!
I assume he was killed during the dog-napping. Oh, my poor baby - he must have been so frightened.
It must have been around 10 or 11. I always visit Apollo during his morning bath time. When I came back after lunch, Marco was dead and Apollo was GONE!
No, I didn't. But, that's Apollo's daily schedule! Only myself and Marco know his schedule - oh, and the competition veterinarian.
Uh...What's her name again? It must be written on the prescription paper somewhere here. She's the only other person who knows Apollo's schedule, but what could she have to do with this?
Anything else?
Who's Apollo?
Do you know what happened here?
What can you tell me about the deceased?
Do you know how Mr. Williams died?
When was the last time you saw Mr. Williams alive?
Did you write this note?
Who is the competition veterinarian?
Thank you for your time.
How can I help you?
I don't think so. We found the body here without much blood, so Dr. Robbins suggested that we search for blood in other places to find out where the death of the victim occurred.
Unless she voluntarily agrees, we will need to get a warrant. Ask her if she agrees to provide samples.
Your tweezers are a good tool to collect evidence like human head hair.
Use your swabs to collect cheek cells.
Try using the impression materials and have people bite on it.
Are you sure? I think there is more work to do here.
I agree. Let's move on.
Do I search for blood here?
How do I collect evidence from Ms. Ranger?
I think I'm done here.
How do I get a hair sample?
How do I get a DNA sample?
How do I get a bite impression?
I'll keep investigating.
I'll select our next place.
Well? What have you learned so far? Have you found my Apollo?
Well, why aren't you out looking for him! Why are you wasting your time here?
Evidence? What kind of evidence? You don't honestly believe I had anything to do with this?
Well, if that's the case, take whatever you need.
Well, alright. Just be gentle, this hairdo cost me a fortune.
Oh, I faint at the sight of blood. I hope you've brought the right tool for a bloodless collection.
You need a what? Well, if it will help to find my Apollo, you're welcome to it.
We haven't found Apollo yet.
May I collect some evidence from you?
What can you tell me about the deceased?
We can use the evidence to help eliminate suspects.
I'll need a hair sample.
I'll need a DNA sample.
I'll need a bite impression.
I'm going to collect the evidence now.
